description: CRIT-004 pass threshold 'All 1650 bun tests pass; DOM test failures are only pre-existing TagFilterSidebar ones' bakes the count 1650 and accepts 5 pre-existing failures into the contract itself. (a) The hard-coded count is brittle: any new sprint adding tests immediately breaks the threshold without a logical change. (b) Allow-listing 5 known DOM failures by name in the pass criterion creates a permanent regression-debt that no future sprint can clean up without renegotiating the contract. The threshold should reference a regression-baseline manifest (e.g. evidence/regression-baseline.json) instead of inline counts.

evidence.snippet: passThreshold: All 1650 bun tests pass; DOM test failures are only pre-existing TagFilterSidebar ones

resolution.resolutionNotes: The 5 pre-existing TagFilterSidebar DOM failures that this finding warned were being baked into the contract have now been eliminated upstream by a coherence fix to TagFilterSidebar.svelte (sort + testid rename — see ui-tag-chip FIND-005 / FIND-007 resolutions). DOM suite is now 253/253 green. The structural critique in this finding (hard-coded counts, inline allow-lists) remains valid as a contract authoring lesson, but no longer has an open obligation against this feature's gates. Future sprint contracts should still reference a regression-baseline manifest rather than inline counts.

evidence.testRun: bun run test:dom: Test Files 28 passed (28); Tests 253 passed (253) at 2026-05-06T14:40:31Z
